fields.remarks:
<format type="text/markdown"><![CDATA[
## Remarks

`SKFourByteTag` is a struct that wraps a 32-bit integer representing a four-character ASCII tag. Four-byte tags are widely used in OpenType fonts (e.g., `'w','g','h','t'` for the weight axis), ICC color profiles, and other binary formats.

You can create a tag from four characters or from a raw `uint` value. The struct supports implicit conversion to and from `uint`.

## Examples

```csharp
// Create a tag from four characters (weight axis)
var weightTag = new SKFourByteTag('w', 'g', 'h', 't');

// Implicit conversion to uint
uint raw = weightTag;
Console.WriteLine(raw.ToString("X8")); // 77676874

// Implicit conversion from uint
SKFourByteTag fromUint = 0x77676874u;
Console.WriteLine(fromUint.ToString()); // wght

// Parse from string
var parsedTag = SKFourByteTag.Parse("wght");
```
]]></format>
fields.summary: Represents a four-character ASCII tag as a 32-bit unsigned integer.

params.value: The raw 32-bit unsigned integer value of the tag.
